question_text: A student drops a ball from 5 metres high. At 2.5 metres high (halfway down), the ball has 200 J of kinetic energy. What is the total mechanical energy of the ball at the start? (Assume air resistance is negligible.)
explanation: At the midpoint, the ball has lost half its original gravitational potential energy (100 J) and gained kinetic energy (200 J). Using conservation: Initial PE = PE at midpoint + KE at midpoint = 200 J + 200 J = 400 J total.
